The green marker click changed the board but didn't complete the level. Let me check what changed. The green marker seems to reverse the rotation (like I expected). Let me look at what changed between the before and after frames.
I need to understand the game mechanics better. Let me check what changed after clicking the top green marker.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after the green click
# Compare previous_frame to current_frame
ascii_before = previous_frame.ascii.split('\n')
ascii_after = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

def get_row_colors(row):
    line = ascii_after[row]
    colors = []
    i = 0
    while i < len(line):
        ch = line[i]
        if ch not in ('c', 'G', 'N', 'B', 'R'):
            j = i
            while j < len(line) and line[j] == ch:
                j += 1
            colors.append(ch)
            i = j
        else:
            i += 1
    return tuple(colors)

print("After top green click:")
for row in [17, 20, 23, 26, 29, 32, 35, 38, 41, 44]:
    colors = get_row_colors(row)
    print(f"  Row {row}: {colors}")

# Check if row 23 has matching pair (p, p)
print(f"\nRow 23 has matching pair: {get_row_colors(23)[0] == get_row_colors(23)[1]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me systematically check for the state where all non-marker rows have matching pairs
# I'll click the top red marker and check after each click

def get_row_colors(frame, row):
    ascii_lines = frame.ascii.split('\n')
    line = ascii_lines[row]
    colors = []
    i = 0
    while i < len(line):
        ch = line[i]
        if ch not in ('c', 'G', 'N', 'B', 'R'):
            j = i
            while j < len(line) and line[j] == ch:
                j += 1
            colors.append(ch)
            i = j
        else:
            i += 1
    return tuple(colors)

def count_matching(frame):
    rows = [20, 23, 29, 32, 38, 41]
    count = 0
    for row in rows:
        c = get_row_colors(frame, row)
        if len(c) >= 2 and c[0] == c[1]:
            count += 1
    # Row 44 has 5 blocks, check if all same
    c44 = get_row_colors(frame, 44)
    if len(c44) >= 2 and all(x == c44[0] for x in c44):
        count += 1
    return count

# Check current state
print(f"Current matching count: {count_matching(current_frame)}/7")

# Try clicking top red marker up to 16 times (cycle length)
for i in range(16):
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 17, 'col': 20}])
    matches = count_matching(current_frame)
    if matches >= 4:
        print(f"After {i+1} clicks: {matches}/7 matching!")
        # Print details
        for row in [20, 23, 29, 32, 38, 41, 44]:
            c = get_row_colors(current_frame, row)
            print(f"  Row {row}: {c}")
    if last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
        print("LEVEL COMPLETED!")
        break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
